Part C · Narration
Age 8
Tell me how a sedimentary rock forms. Start with rain falling on a mountain.
Age 11
Trace a single grain of quartz through the complete rock cycle. At each stage, what assumption is required to estimate how long that stage took?
✦ Faith & Wonder
Every rock on Earth has been through more than we can easily imagine — buried deep, heated, compressed, lifted, worn away, deposited again. The same God who set these cycles in motion sustains them. "Of old hast thou laid the foundation of the earth." — Psalm 102:25

Part B · Reconstruct Lyell's Argument
WHAT LYELL OBSERVED
WHAT LYELL ASSUMED
WHAT LYELL CONCLUDED
Part C · The Key Question
Age 8
Charles Lyell looked at the same rocks as everyone else. He made a choice about what to believe before he started. What was that choice?
Age 11
Lyell said catastrophism makes geology "scientifically uncontrollable." Is this a scientific argument or a philosophical one? What is the difference between a scientific conclusion and a methodological commitment?
✦ A Note on Deep Time
Lyell wrote explicitly in letters that he wanted to "free science from Moses." This is not a creationist claim — it is in his own correspondence. His observations were often excellent. His interpretive framework was shaped by prior commitments, as all frameworks are. The Orthodox Church teaches that God made the world, the Flood was real history, and death entered creation through Adam's sin. We can study Lyell honestly, understand his argument fully, and still hold what God has revealed. How do we know that?

End-of-Unit Oral Assessment
Pick up a rock — what family? How do you know?
What is a fossil and how does one form?
Who was Charles Lyell and what did he claim?
What is the Cambrian Explosion?
What is the difference between an observation and an interpretation?
Age 11 additional:
What is uniformitarianism? What does it assume?
Three assumptions in radiometric dating?
What does the Church teach about death and creation?
